title: 史維：強烈譴責攻擊大陸生行為，將尋求外界協助，並採取適當法律行動

replies.msg:
Dear Members of the HKUST Community:


Regarding the incident during the Open Forum on Nov 6, which included an attack on our UST student, we want to communicate a few points:


1. After the event we have seen many video recordings of the entire incident including the deliberate and totally unacceptable assault and battery on our student. We want to reiterate that no violence on our campus will be tolerated, and we strongly condemn this attack.

2. Disciplining those responsible for this malicious incident is one of our highest priorities. We are seeking external professional help to more effectively collect and review all of the available evidence to identify those responsible for the violence as well as those deliberately shielding the violence from view, and who among them were HKUST students. We are making every effort, and hope that everyone will work with us to help quickly identify those
responsible.

3. Once we have found the attackers and those who assisted them, for those who are HKUST students we will follow the University disciplinary process and impose appropriate punishment, and no matter whether those responsible are HKUST students or not, we will pursue appropriate legal action.

4. Some among our HKUST community have expressed concern over the security arrangements for the Open Forum. We will evaluate all of the security measures as well as the process of handling safety during and following the attack.


Let us join hands to make common cause in restoring and maintaining a safe and peaceful atmosphere on our campus and among our community.

Sincerely,

Wei Shyy, President
Lionel Ni, Provost
Mark Hodgson, Vice-President for Administration and Business
Yang Wang, Dean of Science
Tim Cheng, Dean of Engineering
Kar Yan Tam, Dean of Business and Management
Kellee Tsai, Dean of Humanities and Social Science
King Chow, Director of Interdisciplinary Programs Office cum Acting Dean of Students
Roger Cheng, Associate Provost for Teaching and Learning
Andrew Cohen, Director HKUST Jockey Club Institute for Advanced Study

胡锡进
昨天 12:53 来自 HUAWEI P30 Pro 已编辑
【谁打我们内地的孩子，虽远必惩】香港科技大学郑同学星期三被打之后，港科大校方未做任何表态。星期五，多名港科大的内地生对环球时报表达了他们对安全的担心，表示一些同学已经或者正在考虑暂时回内地规避，还用“大逃亡”来比喻内地生的状况。这样的情形让老胡想到世界一些地方曾经发生过的针对特定人群实施迫害的人道主义灾难。香港现在正在出现这样的苗头。

如今香港高校里的内地学生不敢在有当地学生的场合讲普通话，表达爱国情怀，他们随时可能遭到歧视甚至人身攻击。一些香港激进学生正在形成当年纳粹党员一般的政治狂热，不容忍内地元素在香港社会的正常存在。一段时间以来，内地的机构在香港不断被打砸，内地人被打的情况越来越多。

必须阻止这种攻击内地元素、特别是殴打内地人的歇斯底里。香港高校是内地学子集中、但被香港当地学生包围并处于绝对少数的地方。如果发生对内地人的大规模攻击，香港高校是很危险的潜在爆发点。所以星期三殴打郑同学的事件必须受到公开谴责和处理，它一旦不了了之，就很可能成为港科大和香港其他大学校园内攻击内地生的示范。港科大到现在没有对这起事件做出任何交代，这是完全不可接受的。老胡在此再次表示，如果港科大就是这样的态度，明年内地学生应当完全停止报考港科大。我还呼吁，内地所有对港科大的资助项目都应停止。

同理，香港哪所高校出现对内地学生人身侵犯的事件，都是绝对不可接受的。内地学生和资金都须对那样的香港高校发起坚决的集体抵制。失去了内地的资源，香港的那些高校都将走向衰败。

由于“一国两制”，我们内地社会无法有效影响香港的具体治理，那里喜欢游行闹事，首先是香港人的事。但是他们不能打我们内地人，不能攻击在那里读书的内地孩子。这条底线是必须坚守的。谁打我们的孩子，虽远必惩。

quote.msg:
Dear Members of the HKUST Community:


Regarding the incident during the Open Forum on Nov 6, which included an attack on our UST student, we want to communicate a few points:


1. After the event we have seen many video recordings of the entire incident including the deliberate and totally unacceptable assault and battery on our student. We want to reiterate that no violence on our campus will be tolerated, and we strongly condemn this attack.

2. Disciplining those responsible for this malicious incident is one of our highest priorities. We are seeking external professional help to more effectively collect and review all of the available evidence to identify those responsible for the violence as well as those deliberately shielding the violence from view, and who among them were HKUST students. We are making every effort, and hope that everyone will work with us to help quickly identify those
responsible.

3. Once we have found the attackers and those who assisted them, for those who are HKUST students we will follow the University disciplinary process and impose appropriate punishment, and no matter whether those responsible are HKUST students or not, we will pursue appropriate legal action.

4. Some among our HKUST community have expressed concern over the security arrangements for the Open Forum. We will evaluate all of the security measures as well as the process of handling safety during and following the attack.


Let us join hands to make common cause in restoring and maintaining a safe and peaceful atmosphere on our campus and among our community.

Sincerely,

Wei Shyy, President
Lionel Ni, Provost
Mark Hodgson, Vice-President for Administration and Business
Yang Wang, Dean of Science
Tim Cheng, Dean of Engineering
Kar Yan Tam, Dean of Business and Management
Kellee Tsai, Dean of Humanities and Social Science
King Chow, Director of Interdisciplinary Programs Office cum Acting Dean of Students
Roger Cheng, Associate Provost for Teaching and Learning
Andrew Cohen, Director HKUST Jockey Club Institute for Advanced Study
